sections.title: Introduction to Inversion Questions
sections.content: Inversion is the most formal way to ask questions in French. It involves inverting the order of the subject pronoun and the verb, adding a hyphen between them. This is similar to forming questions in English by changing word order.
examples: Tu parles → Parles-tu? (You speak → Do you speak?)
Il va → Va-t-il? (He goes → Does he go?)
Nous avons → Avons-nous? (We have → Do we have?)

sections.title: Formation with Subject Pronouns
sections.content: With subject pronouns, simply invert the pronoun and verb and add a hyphen. Note that je becomes j' in inversion, and il/elle/on add -t- for pronunciation.

sections.title: Inversion with Être
sections.content: When the verb is être, inversion works the same way. Note that the -t- is added for il/elle/on for pronunciation.

sections.title: Inversion with Avoir
sections.content: Inversion with avoir follows the same pattern as other verbs.

sections.title: Inversion in Different Tenses
sections.content: Inversion can be used in all tenses, though it's more common in present tense for formal questions.
examples: Passé composé: As-tu mangé? (Did you eat?)

sections.title: Inversion with Noun Subjects
sections.content: When the subject is a noun (not a pronoun), you cannot invert. Instead, you must repeat the subject with a pronoun after the verb. This is less common in spoken French.
examples: Marie parle → Marie parle-t-elle? (Does Marie speak?)
Le chat dort → Le chat dort-il? (Is the cat sleeping?)
Les enfants jouent → Les enfants jouent-ils? (Are the children playing?)

sections.title: Formal vs Informal Questions
sections.content: Inversion is more formal. In informal speech, French speakers often use intonation alone (like in English) or est-ce que. Inversion is common in writing and formal speech.
examples: Formal: Parlez-vous français? (Do you speak French?)
Informal: Vous parlez français? (You speak French?) - intonation
Neutral: Est-ce que vous parlez français? (Do you speak French?) - est-ce que

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Transform these statements into inversion questions: 1) Tu es prêt. 2) Il a un livre. 3) Nous allons au cinéma. 4) Vous parlez anglais. 5) Ils ont fini. 6) Elle veut partir. 7) Tu peux m'aider.
